raw_consumer_statement: In XX/XX/XXXX XXXX charged my Best Buy credit account for {$120.00} ; this was for " auto renewing '' computer service that I had already cancelled with XXXX ( this " renewel '' fee of {$120.00} on this credit card never should have happened ). When I called Best Buy they said it was an " unauthorized charge '' and they removed this charge. About a month later they recharged my account claiming this should have been reported as " fraud '' not an " unauthorized charge ''. I told them that was their fault as it was their person who took my info and reported it this way ( not me! ). I then refiled my " fraudulant claim '' and once again, this charge was removed. But then, XXXX replied to Best Buy fraud dept and stated that this was an " auto renewel '' charge ( neglecting to add that I had already cancelled prior to this " renewel '' ) so Best Buy ruled in XXXX 's favor and recharged my credit card {$120.00} on XX/XX/XXXX. I then called XXXX just to tell them what " scammers '' they were but was actually surpirsed when they offered to refund me a " compromised '' amount of {$85.00}. Not wanting to argue anymore I agreed and on XX/XX/XXXX my Best Buy account was credited {$85.00} by XXXX. I then called Best Buy credit card and asked to pay any outstanding balance, which I was told was {$110.00}, and them, close my account, which is what I did. I recently went online to make sure this account was closed and I saw that my account now had a balance due of {$83.00}. I called them and said this is impossible! I was told my balance was {$110.00}, which I paid on XX/XX/XXXX -- how do I now owe another {$83.00}? Upon further research it appears that Best Buy took it upon themselves to re-charge my account the {$85.00} that XXXX gave me credit for. So, only thru my efforts ( and none on Best Buy 's part at all ) did I get XXXX to give me a credit of {$85.00} on XX/XX/XXXX, only to then, on the same day, have Best Buy return it to them and by doing so, recharge me for this amount of {$85.00} again! And now, Best Buy is giving me the run around that " nobody is available to talk to me '' about this " mistake '' and " someone will have to call me back '' ( but no one ever does ). As such, my credit card is now showing a past due balance of {$83.00} ( they did give me a credit for a few dollars for interest that I never should have had to pay, although now, I am probably once again accruing more interest charges and probably late fees, too ( before this situation, I did have " perfect credit ''! ).
